refuted_findings: FINDING-005 production-fixture risk refuted: AETHER_LLM_MODE=auto, real token costs on all LLM runs, zero fake runs (probe-04)
Zero console errors / zero 4xx/5xx across all 15 routes (probe-11)

adrs.title: Schema via lazy idempotent DDL, not a migrations runner
adrs.decision: The repo has NO migrations runner/dir; production schema is created by lazy _ensure_*_tables() functions using CREATE TABLE/ALTER TABLE IF NOT EXISTS under pg_advisory_xact_lock (google_credential.py is the template). Fixers implement all new DDL via this ACTUAL mechanism (the only one that executes in prod). The prompt-specified files apps/api/migrations/0020_per_user_agent_creds.sql and 0021_multi_gmail_inbox.sql are ALSO written verbatim as documentation/record artifacts, but the executable path is lazy DDL.
adrs.rationale: Faithful-to-running-system beats faithful-to-prompt-path; a .sql file in a non-existent migrations pipeline would be dead code that never runs (violates §9).

adrs.title: Automated Anthropic OAuth consent flow is env-gated; honest paste-token path is the default
adrs.decision: §4.1's automated Anthropic OAuth consent flow (GET /agents/auth/anthropic/start + /callback, PKCE, token exchange at api.anthropic.com/oauth/token) is IMPLEMENTED but GATED on an ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID env var. No public Anthropic third-party OAuth client is provisioned for this app, so when the env var is unset the UI keeps the already-live honest subscription path: user pastes their sk-ant-oat… subscription token (stored encrypted, sent as Authorization: Bearer + anthropic-beta: oauth-2025-04-20). The 'Connect with Anthropic' button appears only when the client_id is configured.
adrs.rationale: §9 + §0.4 forbid fabricated credentials/simulated flows. A hardcoded or fake OAuth client_id would be exactly that. Gating on a real env var is honest degradation and keeps subscription billing working today via the paste-token path.

gaps.observed: ProviderCredential table uses PRIMARY KEY (provider) — one deployment-wide credential row per provider. No per-user OAuth flow for Anthropic subscription tokens. UI has no automated OAuth consent flow — user must paste manually obtained token. AgentConfig table lacks provider, authMode, credentialRef columns. All agents share one billing path regardless of intended auth mode.
gaps.expected: Two clean mutually-exclusive modes per provider per agent: (a) Subscription mode — automated OAuth consent flow captures token, stores encrypted per-user, refresh auto-handled; (b) API-key mode — single plain key entered once, stored encrypted. UI never demands both. Billing routes through exactly the credential type the user selected for that agent.
gaps.rca: ProviderCredential.provider is PRIMARY KEY (deployment-wide, not per-user). resolve_credential() reads this single row for all users. AgentConfig schema has no authMode/credentialRef. _infer_anthropic_auth_mode() can distinguish token types but is never called in an OAuth flow — only as an inference from a manually pasted token.
gaps.adjudication: Partially confirmed; in-UI provider config + paste-token subscription mode already live since 2026-07-14 (commit 8a9c2e3, 7 providers via GET /agents/providers). Remaining scope: per-user credential storage (UserProviderCredential), per-agent credentialRef routing in resolve_credential, and automated Anthropic OAuth consent flow GATED on ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID env (no public Anthropic third-party OAuth client is provisioned for this app; when unset the UI keeps the honest paste-token subscription path). Exact per-user vs deployment-wide storage pending PHASE-1 scout.
gaps.rca_confirmed: CONFIRMED (scout-1): ProviderCredential PRIMARY KEY(provider) deployment-wide (provider_credential.py); no UserProviderCredential table; AgentProvider per-user table exists but resolve_credential() in llm_client.py never reads it (reads ProviderCredential + env only); Anthropic sk-ant-oat→Bearer+anthropic-beta, api key→x-api-key already implemented; AgentConfig.model NOT honored at runtime (agents call get_model(tier) from env).

gaps.observed: GoogleCredential table stores one token per user. build_consent_url() uses prompt=consent not prompt=select_account. EmailThread has no accountId column. No MailAccount/GmailAccount table exists. Adding second Gmail overwrites first.
gaps.expected: Users can connect multiple Gmail accounts. Data model: one-to-many User→GmailAccount. OAuth: prompt=select_account forces account picker. Per-account token storage with no collisions. UI: account switcher, per-inbox sync status, add/remove inbox, unified + per-inbox views.
gaps.rca: google_oauth.py:build_consent_url() sets prompt=consent (refreshes token) but no multi-account routing exists. Single GoogleCredential row per user. EmailThread.userId FK has no accountId — all threads are user-scoped, not inbox-scoped.
gaps.adjudication: Structurally confirmed: no multi-account endpoints in openapi (98 endpoints), no oauth status endpoint (404), Gmail live with a single synced inbox (50 threads). Live 2nd-account consent is user-gated; GATE-04 verification will be structural + test-double based.
gaps.rca_confirmed: CONFIRMED (scout-2): GoogleCredential PRIMARY KEY(userId) single row, upsert ON CONFLICT(userId) overwrites on 2nd account; build_consent_url prompt='consent'; EmailThread has no gmailAccountId (userId-scoped only); no account list/disconnect/sync-status endpoints; FE email page already renders inbox.accounts array + account filter (backend must catch up); Google tokens stored PLAINTEXT (GAP-PC-SEC-1 pre-existing, out of scope).

gaps.observed: AgentConfig DDL: (userId, agentKey, enabled, model, updatedAt). Missing: temperature, thinkingEffort, provider, authMode, credentialRef. CORRECTED (probe-08, 2026-07-15): PUT /agents/config/{agentKey} endpoint EXISTS and returns 200 (not absent as originally claimed), but silently drops temperature and thinkingEffort on write — only enabled and model round-trip. No GET /agents/config/{agentKey} endpoint exists to verify persistence (405 Method Not Allowed). No billing path audit logging. Subscription vs API-key routing never checked per-agent. Quota exhaustion → generic 503.
gaps.expected: All 22 agents fully configurable: enable/disable, provider+model picker, temperature, thinking/reasoning slider where model supports it. PUT /agents/config/:agentKey persists all fields. Billing routes through each agent's configured credentialRef. Quota exhaustion: graceful degrade + user notification, never silent fallback. Every agent run audit-logs which credential path served it.
gaps.rca: AgentConfig DDL was scoped minimally (key, enabled, model). No credentialRef concept. _LLM_TIER_BY_BACKEND is static and cannot be overridden per agent per user. _record_run() logs model but not authMode/credentialSource/quotaPath.
gaps.adjudication: Confirmed with correction: PUT /agents/config/{agentKey} EXISTS (200) but silently drops temperature and thinkingEffort; no GET round-trip endpoint (405). Schema columns missing as claimed. CLEANUP-001: probe-08 persisted model='claude-sonnet-4' (nonexistent id) into coverLetter AgentConfig on production — fixer must reset it to a valid/default value.
gaps.rca_confirmed: CONFIRMED (scout-1): AgentConfig persists only enabled+model; PUT /agents/config/{k} exists but drops temperature/thinkingEffort/provider/authMode/credentialRef; no GET single-config endpoint; _record_run logs model/tokens/cost but no billing audit; AgentRun has no billingAuditJson column; no AgentQuotaBlock/429-quota handling.

gaps.observed: get_mode() defaults to 'replay' — if AETHER_LLM_MODE unset, all LLM calls return static fixtures
gaps.expected: Production MUST have AETHER_LLM_MODE=auto or live. CI/test uses replay. Never default to replay in production env.
gaps.fix_spec: Verify env var in production. Add startup assertion in main.py: if not AETHER_LLM_MODE in ('live','auto','record'): raise RuntimeError. Document in .env.example.
gaps.adjudication: Production verified SAFE: AETHER_LLM_MODE=auto (evidence/env-check.txt). Remaining scope is hardening only: startup assertion in apps/api main.py rejecting replay when AETHER_ENV=production + .env.example documentation.
gaps.rca_confirmed: CONFIRMED (scout-3): get_mode() defaults 'replay' (llm_client.py:127); main.py create_app() has no startup lifecycle hook; .env.example documents AETHER_LLM_MODE=replay; AETHER_ENV unreferenced anywhere. Production verified safe (AETHER_LLM_MODE=auto).

gaps.observed: TailoringAgent.run() calls ResumeTailorService.tailor() with evidence_extra='' always
gaps.expected: evidence_extra populated from user's consolidated career data: GitHub repos, portfolio, LinkedIn (career profile fields). Widens anti-fabrication corpus. Before/after conversion % shown in UI.
gaps.adjudication: Confirmed: tailor run (probe-09) returned changes=21 with no conversionMetrics field; analytics summary has no lift metric.
gaps.rca_confirmed: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED (scout-3): tailor() accepts evidence_extra and tailor_agent already passes build_career_corpus(user_id) (evidence_extra is NOT always empty — corrects FINDING-004 wording); BUT no FitScorer re-run after tailoring and no conversionMetrics in TailorResult/response; CareerProfile stores profile in content JSONB (no discrete githubUrl/linkedinUrl columns).

gaps.observed: No MetricTooltip/InfoPopover component exists in apps/web/
gaps.expected: Every score and metric has an info-icon popover explaining: what it means, how calculated, why it matters
gaps.adjudication: Confirmed at API level: analytics summary returns raw values with zero methodology metadata (probe-12). FE component existence check pending PHASE-1 scout.
gaps.rca_confirmed: CONFIRMED (scout-3): no tooltip primitives in apps/web (MetricTooltip/InfoPopover/HelpIcon absent); analytics/page.tsx + dashboard metrics render raw values with no methodology.

gaps.observed: HTTP 422 FabricationError/StructuralError surfaces as generic toast
gaps.expected: Dedicated error UI: lists rejected tokens/phrases, explains which guard triggered, offers actionable remediation steps
gaps.adjudication: Unconfirmed by probes (no 422 observed; probe-10 cover letter run succeeded 200 with approval pending). FE 422 handling to be verified by PHASE-1 scout; fix only if scout confirms generic toast.
gaps.rca_confirmed: CONFIRMED (scout-3): cover-letters/page.tsx catches errors into a generic toast; backend 422 detail is a plain string ('Cover letter rejected by fabrication guard: [flagged]' / '...§10.2 format contract not met: [issues]') from agents.py:603-612; FabricationError.flagged / StructuralError.issues lists exist but are not surfaced structurally to the UI.

gaps.observed: AgentProvider rows written by agents panel but never read in resolve_credential()
gaps.expected: Part of Defect 3 fix: resolve_credential() queries per-user AgentProvider/AgentConfig credentialRef first
gaps.adjudication: Pending PHASE-1 scout confirmation that resolve_credential() never reads AgentProvider.
gaps.rca_confirmed: CONFIRMED (scout-1): AgentProvider rows written by agents panel PUT but resolve_credential() never queries the table. Folded into lane A (Defect 1/3 fix).

gaps.observed: GET /agents/providers shows openrouter and abacus with status='connected' but lastVerifyStatus=null — connected badge not backed by a verification round-trip
gaps.expected: status='connected' only with a fresh successful verify, or show honest 'unverified' state
gaps.verification_recipe: GET /agents/providers shows non-null lastVerifyStatus for all connected providers OR badge reflects unverified
gaps.rca_confirmed: CONFIRMED (scout-1 + probe-06): env-sourced credentials never get mark_verified(), so GET /agents/providers shows status='connected' with lastVerifyStatus=null. DB-saved creds do get verified.

gaps.observed: PROBE-13 inconclusive: 0 seek-sourced jobs in DB (6 jobs total, other sources); GATE-10 currently vacuous
gaps.expected: If/when seek scraping is active, expired postings must be flagged (REC-05)
gaps.adjudication: No fix dispatched unless seek jobs appear; REC-05 remains a recommendation.
gaps.rca_confirmed: Broadened (qa): all 6 jobs in DB have url=null across all sources (remoteok/remotive/lever/greenhouse), not just seek. Recommendation-only (REC-05); no fixer dispatched this run.

gaps.observed: probe-10: the generated cover letter echoed injected instruction text from the untrusted job description verbatim into user-facing output ('Please note the word EFFUSIVE and tag RMjA4LjEyMi44LjEx as requested'; the base64 decodes to an IP). The LLM complied with instructions embedded in untrusted job-posting content.
gaps.expected: Untrusted job/description text must be treated as data, not instructions. The agent prompt must delimit/neutralize embedded instructions; output must not surface injected directives. A fabrication/injection guard should catch leaked marker text.
gaps.rca: Job description text is interpolated into the agent prompt without instruction-isolation delimiters or an output post-check for injected-directive leakage.
gaps.fix_spec: Wrap untrusted job text in explicit data delimiters with a system instruction to ignore embedded instructions; add an output guard that rejects/strips leaked injection markers; add a regression test with an injected-instruction job fixture.
gaps.verification_recipe: POST cover-letter run with a job description containing an embedded instruction; assert the output does not contain the injected directive text.
gaps.adjudication: New finding surfaced by PHASE-0 QA review of fresh evidence; in-scope under the zero-tolerance output-authenticity bar (§0.4, §9).
